summary: Lead accepts the M3 historical claim pipeline after historical_researcher R2 and independent qa_reviewer acceptance. The delivered document is a documentation-only research gate for M3 names, titles, kinship, offices, polity relationships, vassalage assertions, and symbols; it uses existing claim schema/template fields, keeps examples abstract validation-only, and changes no product code or formal historical content.

tests_run.command: node -e scope/encoding check for dirty paths
node -e document marker and abstract-example check

tests_run.result: M3-HISTORICAL-CLAIM-PIPELINE-001 was REVIEW before lead acceptance; all prior integrated tasks remained CLOSED.
M3-POLITY-VASSALAGE-001 is the remaining ready M3 candidate.
format:check, lint, typecheck, and architecture:check passed.

decisions: ACCEPT: M3-HISTORICAL-CLAIM-PIPELINE-001 may be committed and opened as a PR.
The first writer handoff is retained as audit history; R2 is the routed writer handoff accepted by QA.
No downstream M3 implementation task is started by this acceptance.
risks: The pipeline is documentation and policy only; later content-source, compiler, and runtime fixture tasks still need their own enforcement gates.
RESEARCH REQUIRED remains a workflow stop label, not a schema status value.
requested_action: Commit and open the M3 historical claim pipeline PR. Do not start downstream implementation before integration.
